text: Iisɔ yaa weeni ayen, <<Daa wa yuenni sum ale Jekob ya? Wa paasi mu nna wa ka buye. Wa pa m biamu kpaŋtaŋa, yɔgyɔgla a pa m niaka.>> Wa yaa bek ayen, <<Fi an tali niaka a magsi mi?>>

text: Basi ate jinla m va fi duŋsaŋa po a yik pa sobta ale bua ŋaai nyiŋsaŋa ale ŋmirisi la, ate ŋa chim m tutuna.
translated: So if someone wants to do something but does not have the strength or ability to do it, then it will not be possible for him.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob bought bread with almond bread and honey bread and gave them to his friends to eat, he bought enough so that everyone could get a piece.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Wa yaa weeni ba ayen, <<Mi nya kama ayen ni kowa nyeelaŋa ale mu a kan nye ase ku ale jam ka diila, alege m kowa Wenni a bo ale mu kama.
translated: If you do something good for someone, "I know that person will remember you and when you are in need or in trouble, they will also help you."

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Laban ale nyini a ga boro a gebi wa piisaŋa kɔbtaŋa la, Arekel yaa zu wa kowa yenni wenaŋa.
translated: If you want to go to the farm early in the morning, Arekel will go with you so you won't be alone.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: M ta pagrim a baga a nye fu wari kama. Alege yokku po, fi kowa Wenni a weeni mu kama ayen. Be seba ate fi kan weeni biem wari yaase nalim wari a te Jekob.
translated: He told them a story about how people used to share food. Long ago, on a certain day, Wenni and his friends went to share food together. They sat down and each person took a portion of food from what Jacob had prepared.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Arekel yaa weeni wa kowa ayen, <<M nyɔnɔ, kan te fi sui a puuri ale mu m ni kan baga yiti a zaani fi niŋ la. M boka m chiak lɔka po.>> Ate wa gisi alege wa an baga a nya yenni wenaŋa.
translated: The child said to his mother, "Mother, give me some of the food you are cooking so that I can eat with my friends. I am very hungry." The mother looked at him and then gave him some food to eat with his friends.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Tintomdɔma de ale ŋman cheŋ Jekob jigi la, ba weeni wa kama ayen. Ti cheŋ fi suoku Iisɔ jigi kama ate wa cheena ale nuruba kobsinaansi ayen ba tu fu suik.
translated: The descendants of Jacob who came, were seventy in all. Joseph was already in Egypt, and then his brothers and all their families joined him there.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Wa bo ka dula yokku meena, ate wa le ta ŋan baai la, wa yaa yeeri ŋa po ate ku chim tiirim a ta cheŋ ga te wa suoku Iisɔ.
translated: They sat together for a long time, and when it was getting dark, they took the child and went home before it became too late.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Wa yaa weeni waai le dɛ niŋ la ayen, <<Ase m suoku Iisɔ a dan tu fu suik a bek fu ayen, <Ka wana ale soa fi, fi be a cheŋ ka bɛ, ka wana ale soa duŋsaŋa de meena ale bo fi niŋ la?>
translated: Jesus answered him, "Truly I say to you, unless one is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God." Nicodemus said to him, "How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother's womb and be born?"

translated: Do not be afraid, O people of Israel, for I will help you, says the Lord; I am your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Fi yaa weeni wa ayen, <Ka fi tomteerɔwa Jekob ale soa. Ŋa ka tiirim ate wa pai tom ayen m ta jam te m nyɔnɔwa Iisɔ, wa abe bo ka ti ŋaaŋ a cheena.> >>
translated: But the descendants of Jacob will be like fire. They will destroy the people of Esau, and no one from the people of Esau will survive.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Nuruwa yaa bek wa ayen, <<Fi yuei ale boa?>> Wa yaa tulisi ayen, <<Jekob.>>

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Dila nyiŋ a jam paari jinla Izirali demma a kan ŋɔbi lam buui ale ka jaab kɔsuk la, diila Jekob kɔsukku ate wa jam tiri la nyiŋ.
translated: He brought out his people with joy, his chosen ones with singing. He gave them the lands of the nations, and they took possession of the fruit of others' labor.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.

translated: When they were on their way, Jacob's son became tired and sat down under a big tree to rest.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Jekob yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a saalim fu, wi fu yuei a sak mu.>> Alege wa tulisi wa ayen, <<Ka boan nyiŋ ate fi a bek mi yuei ya?>> Ate wa yaa niak wa dula.
translated: Jacob went to his mother and said, "I am hungry, please give me some food." Then his mother replied, "Why didn't you tell me before you became hungry?" Then they both sat and ate together.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Alege Iisɔ yaa weeni wa ayen, <<M suowa tanla fi ŋantaŋa, mi mɛ dek a ta ŋanta kama nna yegayega.
translated: If you ask me for advice, I will tell you what I know, but I do not know everything.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.

translated: They took their children and their wives and their animals and their things and went to another place to look for a better life.

text: Alege ba tulisi wa ayen, <<Ti be basi ate wa nye ti toawa ase waai ale pai wadek a te nidoaba a tuesi ŋanta la ya?>>
translated: They said that if you want to go with us, you should tell your parents so that they know where you are going.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Iisɔ jam faari wa pooma nyini ka Keenan nipooma po. Ba jam le Ada, Elon Yit denɔwa lie ale Oyolibama Anaya liewa wa jam ka Zibeon Yivi denɔwa ŋaaŋ.
translated: The children of the wife of Keenan were Po. The children of Ada, Elon the Hittite, and Oholibamah Anah the daughter of Zibeon the Hivite.

text: Jekob jam bo ka tɛŋ kaai ate wa kowa jam boro la, Keenan tɛŋka.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Wa suataŋa yaa weeni ayen, <<Fi a yaali ayen fi a dɛ naam tama zuk kama? Fi le sum a nya tama zuwa? Ate ba yaa ŋmasi kisi wa yega wa gɔdaasimaŋa nyiŋ ale wa le weeni diila nyiŋ.
translated: When you hear this story, "What do you think about what happened there? Do you understand what happened there? If you don't, ask your parents or elders so they can explain the story to you."

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.

translated: Israel said to his son Joseph, "Your brothers are pasturing the flock at Shechem. Come, I will send you to them."

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Wa yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a gisi ka m suataŋa. Fi baga a dak ba le ta ba piisaŋa a bo juijui ate ŋa a dɛ la?>>
translated: He/she said to me, "I want to go to the market. Can you tell me if there is anyone selling oranges there?"

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Wa nidoabilisaŋa ale wa liema a jam ayen ba yik wa ate wa sui yɔk, alege wa zɛri. Wa weeni ayen, <<Aaya, mi a kum kama ayen m ga siŋ vɔrup a cheŋ m biika jigi.>> Dila wa kowa a yaa kum wa biika.
translated: They gathered together and discussed what to do so that they could find food, but they were worried. One of them said, "No, I cannot eat unless I see the face of the person who cooked the food." Then they all agreed not to eat the food.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.

text: Diila ba fɛ mi kama a ta nyini Yiburu demma tɛŋka a nyini, ate dela mɛ mi an nye wari ya a magsi ba a nyo mu likka dɔk po.>>
translated: If you want to go to Yiburu, you have to get money so that you can pay for the car fare.

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: Ku saluiku wa popola yaa ta wa a daani, ate wa tom wi sampoporisaŋa ale yam nyama meena Ejipiti tɛŋka po. Faaro yaa weeni ba wa gɔdaasimaŋa alege waai a karo a weeni ŋa kiri a sak wa.
translated: When we were slaves, we cried out to the Lord, and he heard our voice and saw our suffering in Egypt. Pharaoh was cruel to us, but the Lord brought us out with his strong hand.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.
text: Ate wie a sum tulim nye a magsi chaab ale wa ale weeni ti diila. Mi ŋman pilim nya m kpaŋtaŋa kama, ate nuru nwala ba pai wa a yik tiib nyiŋ.>>
translated: When you go to the farm and see a snake, do not be afraid. Just quietly go back the way you came, because the ancestors say that seeing a snake is a sign of good luck.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Josefi yaa tulisi Faaro ayen, <<Mi an baga a nye, alege Wen ale te Faaro weeni ŋaai ate wa a yaali la.>>
translated: Joseph said to Pharaoh, "It is not I; God will give Pharaoh the answer he desires."

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Alege ŋa le poom a nɛ ŋa a nueri la, waai an baga a weeni ayen ŋa nɛ jaaboa. Ŋa lɔrima ase ka ŋa le poom ka diila. Ate mi yaa yiti.
translated: If you see a snake on the road, don't try to kill it unless it is dangerous. Sometimes it is better to leave it and go. That is my advice.
